tasks.description: FEAT-045 [3/3]: Comment count badges on canvas nodes + workspace-level comments view page
acceptance_criteria: Comment count badge on step, section, touchpoint, and stage canvas nodes (only when count > 0)
Badge shows count of top-level comments (parent_id === null) per entity
Canvas views fetch all workspace comments once, build Map<commentable_id, count>, pass through buildNodes to node data
Comments page lists all workspace comments, filterable by category and commentable_type
Each comment shows: entity type+name context, author, category badge, content, resolved state, relative timestamp
Comments nav item in sidebar (MessageCircle icon from lucide-react)
'comments' added to reserved path segments in workspace-shell.tsx

similar_files: src/components/panels/comment-panel.tsx — CATEGORY_CONFIG with colors/labels for badges
patterns_to_follow: Annotation indicator: annotatedIds Set built from fetchAnnotations, passed through buildNodes → annotationColor on node data → conditional render in node component. Use same flow for commentCounts Map.
buildNodes in flow-canvas.tsx (lines ~83-107): receives annotatedIds/annotationColor, maps over entities to build Node[]. Add commentCounts param, lookup count per entity.
Node data types (canvas.ts): StepNodeData has annotationColor?: string | null. Add commentCount?: number to all 4 types.
Badge placement: annotation dot is absolute positioned top-right. Place comment badge at bottom-right or inline with existing indicators to avoid collision.
Sidebar NAV_ITEMS: array of { label, icon, href } objects. Add after Compare, before Teams. Use MessageCircle from lucide-react.
Reserved paths in workspace-shell.tsx: array used to determine if path is a tab ID or a named route. Add 'comments'.
View page: server page.tsx extracts workspaceId → passes to client view. Client view uses useState + useEffect + fetchComments.
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — fetchComments returns Comment[] directly
React state immutability — use [...arr].sort() not arr.sort()
React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)
affected_urls: /w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] — canvas nodes show comment count badges

tasks.special_instructions: Canvas badge approach: In canvas-view.tsx and journey-canvas-view.tsx, fetch all workspace comments once via fetchComments(workspace.id). Build a Map<string, number> counting only top-level comments (parent_id === null) per commentable_id. Pass this map to buildNodes in flow-canvas.tsx. In buildNodes, look up count for each entity and set commentCount on node data. In each node component, render a small pill badge (MessageCircle icon + count) only when commentCount > 0. Use subtle styling — match the muted indicator pattern, not the prominent status badge. For the workspace comments page: model after teams-view.tsx. Fetch all comments, display in card list with category badges (reuse CATEGORY_CONFIG color scheme from comment-panel.tsx). Add filter bar with category dropdown and entity type dropdown. Show entity context (e.g. 'Step: Review Application') as clickable link. Sidebar placement: after Compare, before Teams.

tasks.description: Color picker keyboard accessibility + ARIA in perspective settings
acceptance_criteria: Color picker toggle button has aria-expanded={showColorPicker} and aria-haspopup='listbox'
Color picker popup container has role='listbox'
Each preset color button has role='option' and aria-selected matching current perspective color
Escape key closes the picker and returns focus to toggle button
Arrow keys (Left/Right/Up/Down) navigate between color options in the 4-column grid
Enter/Space on focused color selects it and closes picker
Focus moves to currently-selected color (or first) when picker opens

patterns_to_follow: PerspectiveRow color picker: toggle button (line ~392) + conditional popup (lines ~398-416) with 8 preset colors in 4-column grid.
Existing outside-click listener (lines ~368-377) via colorRef + useEffect. Add onKeyDown alongside.
PERSPECTIVE_COLORS array defines the 8 colors. Grid is 4 columns × 2 rows. Arrow navigation: Left/Right = ±1, Up/Down = ±4, wrap at boundaries.
Track focusedIndex with useState. Use useRef array for color button refs. Call ref.current?.focus() on arrow key.
learnings_relevant: React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null) not bare useRef()
WCAG 2.1 Level A: keyboard operability + proper ARIA

tasks.special_instructions: Minimal change — only modify the PerspectiveRow component's color picker section (~30 lines of additions). Do NOT refactor the rest of settings page. Add: (1) aria-expanded and aria-haspopup to toggle button, (2) role='listbox' on popup div, (3) role='option' + aria-selected + tabIndex on color buttons, (4) onKeyDown handler for Escape/Arrow/Enter, (5) useState for focusedIndex, (6) useEffect to focus correct button when picker opens. The 8 colors are in 4-col × 2-row grid: arrows navigate accordingly.

shared_files.note: canvas.ts is owned by slot 1 (adds commentCount to node data types). database.ts and client.ts have Comment type and fetchComments — no modifications needed.
